title: N28: Bridge OpenClaw Sessions to ChatStore Agent Mode
description:
Create bidirectional bridge between native_session_manager and ChatStore.

GOAL: OpenClaw sessions appear as Agent Mode threads in main Chat UI (ChatView), making OpenClawControlUI redundant.

ARCHITECTURE:
- Rust: native_session_manager.rs emits session changes via SSE
- API: 7-apps/api/src/session_routes.rs adds sync endpoints  
- TypeScript: SessionSyncClient bridges to ChatStore.ts
- Result: OpenClaw sessions = Agent Mode threads in ShellRail

BACKEND CHANGES:
1. Add GET /api/v1/sessions/sync - SSE stream of session changes
2. Add POST /api/v1/sessions/:id/bridge - Link session to ChatStore thread
3. Add subscribe_to_changes() to native_session_manager.rs

FRONTEND CHANGES:
1. Create session-bridge.ts with SessionSyncClient class
2. Modify ChatStore.ts: syncWithOpenClaw(), auto-create agent threads
3. Map OpenClaw session IDs to ChatThread IDs with mode='agent'
close_reason: Bridge implementation complete. Files: session_sync.rs (295), session-bridge.ts (518), use-session-bridge.ts (163). SSE endpoint, bidirectional sync, Agent Mode threads working. OpenClawControlUI now deprecated.

title: N03: Design single-binary distribution strategy
description:
Design self-contained single-binary distribution for A2R platform matching Moltis one binary, no runtime approach.

A2R currently has 80+ crates and Python services. Need unified distribution approach for enterprise deployment.

Challenges:
- 80+ Rust crates to link
- Python services (voice, operator)
- Web UI assets
- WASM capsules
- Cross-platform builds

Research Topics:
- Moltis distribution approach (cargo-deb, cargo-bundle)
- rust-embed for asset embedding
- PyOxidizer for Python services
- Static linking strategies
acceptance_criteria: Research Moltis build configuration;Evaluate asset embedding options;Design Python service bundling;Define release artifact structure;Plan cross-platform CI/CD;Estimate binary sizes
close_reason: Completed single-binary distribution strategy RFC. Output: docs/rfcs/single-binary-distribution.md. Key decisions: Use cargo-deb/rpm for packaging, PyOxidizer for Python services, rust-embed for assets. 8-week implementation plan. Estimated binary size: 88MB compressed.
